amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Autophagy in S. cerevisiae is a conserved catabolic process that delivers cytoplasmic components to the vacuole for degradation and recycling. The process involves formation of double-membrane autophagosomes that engulf cargo, followed by fusion with the vacuole and breakdown of contents. The core machinery includes the Atg1 kinase complex for initiation, the PI3K complex for nucleation, two ubiquitin-like conjugation systems (Atg12-Atg5 and Atg8-PE), and the Atg9 cycling system. Autophagy is induced by nutrient starvation, rapamycin treatment, or other stresses through TOR kinase inhibition. This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from autophagy induction through phagophore assembly, cargo selection, autophagosome completion, vacuolar fusion, and cargo degradation.

graph TD
    A[Environmental Stress] -->|Nutrient Starvation| B{TOR Kinase Activity?}
    A -->|Rapamycin| B
    B -->|Active| C[\Autophagy Repressed/]
    B -->|Inactive| D[\Autophagy Induction/]
    
    D --> E[Atg1 Kinase Complex]
    E --> F[Atg1 Atg13 Atg17 Atg29 Atg31]
    F --> G{Atg1 Activation?}
    G -->|Dephosphorylated Atg13| H[Active Atg1 Complex]
    G -->|Phosphorylated| I[\Inactive State/]
    
    H --> J[PAS Pre-Autophagosomal Structure]
    J --> K{Membrane Source?}
    K -->|ER| L[ER-Derived Membranes]
    K -->|Mitochondria| M[MAM Contacts]
    K -->|Golgi| N[Golgi Vesicles]
    
    L --> O[Membrane Nucleation]
    M --> O
    N --> O
    
    O --> P[PI3K Complex I]
    P --> Q[Vps34 Vps15 Atg6 Atg14]
    Q --> R{PI3P Generation?}
    R -->|Active| S[PI3P Enrichment at PAS]
    R -->|Inactive| T[\No Nucleation/]
    
    S --> U[PROPPIN Recruitment]
    U --> V[Atg18 Atg21]
    V --> W[Membrane Curvature]
    
    H --> X[Atg9 Cycling System]
    X --> Y[Atg9 Vesicles]
    Y --> Z[Atg2-Atg18 Complex]
    Z --> AA[Lipid Transfer to PAS]
    
    AA --> AB{Phagophore Expansion?}
    W --> AB
    AB -->|Continuous| AC[/Growing Phagophore/]
    
    S --> AD[\{Ubiquitin-like Conjugation?/]}
    AD -->|System 1| AE[Atg12-Atg5 Pathway]
    AD -->|System 2| AF[Atg8-PE Pathway]
    
    AE --> AG[Atg7 E1-like Enzyme]
    AG --> AH[Atg12 Activation]
    AH --> AI[/Atg10 E2-like Enzyme/]
    AI --> AJ[Atg12~Atg5 Conjugate]
    AJ --> AK[Atg16 Binding]
    AK --> AL[Atg12-Atg5-Atg16 Complex]
    
    AF --> AM[Atg4 Protease]
    AM --> AN[Atg8 Processing]
    AN --> AO[Atg8-I Exposed Glycine]
    AO --> AP[Atg7 E1-like]
    AP --> AQ[Atg3 E2-like]
    AQ --> AR[Atg8-PE Lipidation]
    
    AL --> AS{E3-like Activity?}
    AR --> AS
    AS -->|Enhanced| AT[/Atg8-PE on Membranes/]
    
    AT --> AU[Phagophore Membrane Expansion]
    AU --> AV{Cargo Selection?}
    
    AV -->|Non-selective| AW[Bulk Autophagy]
    AV -->|Selective| AX[Cargo Receptors]
    
    AX --> AY{Selective Autophagy Type?}
    AY -->|Mitophagy| AZ[Atg32 Receptor]
    AY -->|Pexophagy| BA[Atg30 Receptor]
    AY -->|Cvt Pathway| BB[Atg19 Receptor]
    
    AZ --> BC[Mitochondria Sequestration]
    BA --> BD[/Peroxisome Sequestration/]
    BB --> BE[Ape1 Precursor Sequestration]
    
    AW --> BF[Cytoplasm Engulfment]
    BC --> BF
    BD --> BF
    BE --> BF
    
    BF --> BG{Autophagosome Closure?}
    BG -->|Atg8-PE Removal| BH[Atg4 Delipidation]
    BH --> BI[Sealed Autophagosome]
    
    BI --> BJ{Vacuole Fusion?}
    BJ -->|SNARE Complex| BK[Vam3 Vam7 Vti1]
    BJ -->|Rab GTPase| BL(\1)
    BJ -->|Tethering| BM[HOPS Complex]
    
    BK --> BN{Fusion Machinery Assembly?}
    BL --> BN
    BM --> BN
    BN -->|Complete| BO[Membrane Fusion]
    
    BO --> BP[Autophagic Body in Vacuole]
    BP --> BQ[Vacuolar Hydrolases]
    BQ --> BR[/Cargo Degradation?/]
    BR -->|Proteases| BS[Pep4 Prb1]
    BR -->|Lipases| BT[Lipid Breakdown]
    
    BS --> BU[Amino Acid Release]
    BT --> BV[Fatty Acid Release]
    
    BU --> BW[Nutrient Recycling]
    BV --> BW
    BW --> BX[Cellular Metabolism]
    
    BX --> BY{Cell Survival?}
    BY -->|Nutrients Restored| BZ[TOR Reactivation]
    BY -->|Continued Starvation| CA[Sustained Autophagy]
    
    BZ --> CB[Autophagy Shutdown]
    CB --> C
    
    CA --> D

name: Yeast Autophagy
notes: Created based on comprehensive research on S. cerevisiae autophagy, the model system for understanding this conserved eukaryotic process.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive peer-reviewed literature on yeast autophagy, including studies on Atg protein functions, autophagosome biogenesis, and regulatory mechanisms. The pathway components, assembly sequence, and logic gates have been validated against primary research sources. S. cerevisiae autophagy serves as the paradigm for understanding this process in all eukaryotes.
